sessions.title: Y-Sorting and Scene Loading Refactor
sessions.body: Added trader cycle offer sound effect. Began y-sorting integration. One way or another this caused the scene loading system to absolutely shit itself. Had to rip out the redundant, half-used (unintentionally) SceneManager system. Main is now the solo scene loader and game manager. Y-sorting is roughed in, trees are shifting alpha when player walks behind but sorting still needs tweaking. Cut down to 2 forest variants — others were bloaty and would need serious refactoring. Left for a future session.

sessions.title: Y-Sort Pushed to All WorldObjects
sessions.body:
Y-sorting polish completed.
New GroundYSort contains all world props, enemies, trees, etc. Allows for proper y-sorting and clean project organisation.
Y-Sorting implemented for all world objects, all scenes set up woth consistent tree structure.
Key scripts have been cleaned up, general housekeeping of class organisation and method organisation.
Added "Death Silhouette" polish. When the player dies, to add to the lore of the game. As the player is waking up from death at bed spawn. You can see a dark sihouette walking toward the boundary of the forest. The identity is hidden for the demo. The player cannot catch the silhouette.
Tweaked the timing and delay settings of the cutscene, feels good now.
Z-index housekeeping. Text/thoughtbubbles was rendering under boundary tiles, etc. Now redners on +5 z-index.

gdd.title: Concept and Vision
gdd.body: Evergreen is a pixel art survival game in Godot 4.5 using C#.

gdd.title: Core Mechanics
gdd.body:
Stamina-based combat (5 stamina, 2 per attack)
Sleep-based health regeneration
Food and hunger system

gdd.title: Enemies
gdd.body:
Boar — doubles as food source
Thornweaver — area-control thorns

gdd.title: Demo Scope
gdd.body: Define what is and is not in the demo here...
